semantic_definition: control-plane navigation mode selected by PX4
limitations: nav_state does not prove target data path installation

semantic_definition: external component registration result and assigned IDs
collection_method: collector records reply name, success, mode_id, executor_id, arming_check_id
timestamp_source: PX4 stamps reply with hrt_absolute_time; DDS receive time also recorded
writer_attribution: registration name identifies component control plane only
family_a_applicability: Dynamic External Mode and Mode Executor
limitations: unregistration is a request; timeout removal needs vehicle_status and arming-check evidence

semantic_definition: arbitration outcome authorized to command the primary path
topic_or_event: vehicle_status plus vehicle_control_mode plus registration reply
collection_method: RouteEventReducer joins nav_state, control flags, and assigned external mode ID
writer_attribution: component name is retained for registered modes
limitations: Offboard has no registered component identity; derivation is invalid without matching data-plane events

semantic_definition: concrete node or PX4 module producing authority-bearing setpoints
collection_method: join producer-side identity/sequence log to PX4 consumption trace
timestamp_source: producer monotonic timestamp and PX4 boot timestamp require a run clock bridge
writer_attribution: ROS producer is explicit; PX4 consumer is explicit after patch
family_a_applicability: P0 Offboard and Dynamic External Mode
limitations: DDS/uORB do not natively preserve ROS node identity; multi-producer Offboard requires an additional correlation token

semantic_definition: active abstraction level such as position, velocity, attitude, rate, or torque
collection_method: RouteEventReducer maps enabled control flags and registered setpoint type
limitations: flags show configured control levels, not which of multiple fresh topics won on every sample

collection_method: collector combines configured setpoint type with receipt and patched consumption events
timestamp_source: PX4 overwrites zero input timestamp at DDS ingress; consumption uses PX4 boot time
writer_attribution: patched consumption identifies the consuming module
limitations: other setpoint types require equivalent instrumentation before broader experiments

semantic_definition: age of the authority-bearing message at PX4 consumption
collection_method: collector computes max(0, consume_timestamp-subject_timestamp) in a single PX4 domain
timestamp_source: PX4 boot microseconds after DDS ingress timestamp rewrite
limitations: producer-to-DDS latency is separate and needs ROS/DDS receive stamps plus offset model

semantic_definition: controller modules required and enabled by the selected route
collection_method: collector maps flags to mc_pos_control, mc_att_control, mc_rate_control, control_allocator
writer_attribution: module identity derives from documented flag mapping
limitations: a running process may be scheduled but not influence the sample; patched data events strengthen the derivation

semantic_definition: nominal controller stages intentionally skipped by route configuration
collection_method: collector takes the complement of the expected multicopter route modules
limitations: complement is profile-specific and must be recalculated for non-multicopter or direct-actuator routes

semantic_definition: topic and module feeding control allocation
collection_method: patched structured event records vehicle_torque_setpoint and mc_rate_control writer
timestamp_source: PX4 boot microseconds and subject uORB timestamp
limitations: patch covers instance 0 classical path; VTOL instance 1 and direct torque publishers need added IDs

timestamp_source: PX4 boot microseconds and actuator_motors subject timestamp
limitations: native uORB instance and ULog do not preserve publisher identity; other direct writers are outside this patch

semantic_definition: actual commanded actuator values and validity
collection_method: collector records finite channel count, min, and max; ULog retains full vector
writer_attribution: paired patched writer event supplies identity
limitations: actuator_outputs may reflect driver/simulator timing later than actuator_motors

semantic_definition: active PX4 failure flags and top-level failsafe state
collection_method: collector stores active flag names and vehicle_status failsafe
limitations: flag publication proves detection state, not complete fallback installation

semantic_definition: route selected to replace a failed or released primary route
collection_method: collector records first internal nav_state selected after failure/release and then evaluates module/writer installation
writer_attribution: writer evidence comes from subsequent patched route events
family_a_applicability: Offboard loss, External Mode loss, executor RTL/Hold
limitations: selected nav_state alone is insufficient; fallback_target remains provisional until required data-plane events appear
